questions.question: Why did Griffin become a homeless wanderer?
questions.answer: Griffin became homeless because he had to remove his clothes to remain invisible and had no money or shelter.

questions.question: Explain why the footprints without feet created panic.
questions.answer: The footprints appeared without any visible person, creating fear and confusion among people.

questions.question: Why did Griffin find winter unbearable?
questions.answer: Winter was unbearable because Griffin could not wear clothes to protect himself from the cold.

questions.question: How did Griffin manage to survive inside the departmental store?
questions.answer: He stole food, clothes, and wine from the store and slept there at night.

questions.question: Why did Griffin rob the theatrical shop?
questions.answer: He robbed the shop to get money and materials for disguise.

questions.question: Why was Mrs Hall initially sympathetic to Griffin?
questions.answer: She believed he was an eccentric scientist and he paid rent in advance.

questions.question: What made the clergyman’s burglary unusual?
questions.answer: The money was stolen though no intruder was visible.

questions.question: Describe the strange events that occurred in Griffin’s room.
questions.answer: Furniture moved on its own and the room appeared locked and empty.

questions.question: Why did villagers begin to distrust Griffin?
questions.answer: His strange behaviour and sudden possession of money raised suspicion.

questions.question: Why did Griffin reveal his invisibility?
questions.answer: He revealed it out of anger and frustration when questioned.

questions.question: How did Mr Jaffers react to Griffin’s invisibility?
questions.answer: He was shocked but tried to arrest Griffin despite the difficulty.

questions.question: Why was Griffin unable to live peacefully?
questions.answer: His invisibility isolated him and his behaviour made him an enemy of society.

questions.question: What does Griffin’s disguise suggest about his condition?
questions.answer: It suggests his fear of being exposed.

questions.question: Why is Griffin described as lawless?
questions.answer: He commits crimes like theft and violence without remorse.

questions.question: How does society react to the unknown in the story?
questions.answer: Society reacts with fear and suspicion.

questions.question: What role does fear play in the story?
questions.answer: Fear drives the actions of both Griffin and the villagers.

questions.question: Why does Griffin misuse science?
questions.answer: He lacks ethical responsibility and self-control.

questions.question: What lesson does the villagers’ fear convey?
questions.answer: Fear of the unknown can lead to panic and injustice.

questions.question: Why does Griffin fail despite his intelligence?
questions.answer: He fails because intelligence without morality is destructive.

questions.question: What does the title ‘Footprints Without Feet’ signify?
questions.answer: It signifies mystery created by invisibility.

questions.question: How does invisibility isolate Griffin?
questions.answer: It separates him from normal human interaction.

questions.question: What is the central conflict of the story?
questions.answer: The conflict between scientific power and moral responsibility.

questions.question: Why does Griffin lose public sympathy?
questions.answer: His violent and criminal behaviour alienates society.

questions.question: What warning does the story give scientists?
questions.answer: Scientific discoveries must be guided by ethics.

questions.question: Why is Griffin a tragic figure?
questions.answer: His genius is destroyed by moral failure.
